title: Verify email — expired token: expired message shown, email input and Resend button visible, tap Resend → success message

preconditions.description: Expo web app is running and reachable at the configured base URL.
Backend is running. GET /auth/verify-email returns TOKEN_EXPIRED for expired tokens. POST /auth/resend-verification works.

An expired verification token is available in DATA-1. Obtain by: (1) creating a test account with a very short token TTL in the backend dev config, or (2) using a previously-used/expired token from a prior test run.

testData.name: expiredToken
testData.description: An expired email verification token that causes the backend to return TOKEN_EXPIRED.

testData.name: resendEmail
testData.description: Email address to enter into the resend field on the expired state screen. Does not need to match the original signup email for this UI test.

journey.description: Navigate to verify-email with an expired token.
Wait for the GET /auth/verify-email call to return TOKEN_EXPIRED.
Enter email address to receive a new verification link.

assertions.description: Backend returns 400 (or 410) with TOKEN_EXPIRED error code.

target.text: Enter your email address to receive a new verification link.

assertions.description: Instructional text below heading is visible.
Email input field is visible in the expired state.
Resend button is visible in the expired state.
Back to Login link is visible as a secondary action.
Resend API call is made when button is tapped.

target.text: Verification email sent. Check your inbox.

assertions.description: resendStatus === 'sent' renders the green success message.

manualValidationRequired.reason: No supported native automation runner is currently configured.
No supported native automation runner is currently configured.

notes: The expired token must cause the backend to return error code 'TOKEN_EXPIRED'. Any other error code will result in the 'invalid' state, not the 'expired' state.

If obtaining a genuinely expired token is difficult in CI, consider: (1) backend test utility to create an already-expired token, or (2) mocking the network response in the Playwright spec using page.route() to intercept the GET /auth/verify-email call and return a TOKEN_EXPIRED response.
Validation note: if DATA-2 (resendEmail) does not correspond to an actual account, the backend may return an error. Use an actual existing account email for DATA-2 to get a 200 response.
